mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This trend signal is always active.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are negatively correlated with RSAFS monthly growth or Rank IC is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.5 percentage points over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward tests show that such UNRATE increases are followed by positive SPX returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds the year-over-year inflation rate (CPIAUCSL), real interest rates turn positive, tightening financial conditions and depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days. The interaction determines the degree of restrictiveness.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS exceeds CPI inflation, the forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative or are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (GDPC1 growing above trend), accelerating GDP growth signals overheating and potential monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism is only active in expansion regimes.
failure_condition: If in expansion regimes with high GDP growth, 20-day forward returns are not negative more than 60% of the time over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive two-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals contraction in manufacturing and supply chain stress, leading to negative corporate earnings surprises and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If two-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in housing starts (HOUST) above the 12-month moving average signals strong residential investment, boosting construction employment and related sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST above its 12-month MA does not produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of cases over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and real retail sales (RSAFS) are in the top quartile of their recent distributions, the economy risks overheating, prompting monetary tightening expectations that depress equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m growth > 0.3% and RSAFS m/m growth > 1%
failure_condition: If joint high growth in both series does not lead to negative forward returns in at least 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth above 3%), robust payroll employment gains (PAYEMS) fuel wage-price spiral fears, prompting aggressive monetary tightening expectations that depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If during high inflation regimes, strong payroll growth is followed by positive SPX returns more than 40% of the time over a rolling 5-year window.
